name: International Bus Specialist
recent_news: No web presence or news found; PO Box-only listing in Newark, DE with 4 employees and ~$244K revenue suggests very small or dormant operation.
fit_notes: Sub-scale single-operator in DE with minimal digital footprint; likely below Summit's tuck-in threshold and may not be a true active operator. Direct phone outreach needed to verify status.

name: N & J Bus Svc LTD
recent_news: Held five-year transportation contract with Mercer School District through 2020-21 school year (~$191K in 2017); 2018 board controversy noted no competing bidders. USDOT 2055581, intrastate non-hazmat carrier with 8 trucks/8 drivers.
fit_notes: Single-district micro-operator in remote northern WI (Iron County); sole-bidder dynamic suggests entrenched local incumbent but too small for Summit absent regional roll-up strategy in WI.

name: Milwaukee Bus Co Inc
recent_news: Listed at 2761 S Chase Ave, Milwaukee; minimal digital presence. Milwaukee-area school transport dominated by Lamers, Johnson School Bus, GO Riteway, and Lakeside Buses; this entity appears very small or possibly inactive.
fit_notes: Sub-scale Milwaukee-area operator without identifiable principals or customer base; verification call needed before any outreach. Not a fit at current scale.

name: Stenerson Buses Inc
recent_news: Family-owned school bus contractor based in Hillsboro, WI (Vernon County); industry directory pegs sales volume around $296K with ~8 staff. No recent M&A or news coverage found.
fit_notes: Small rural WI family operator likely serving a single district; potential succession candidate given family name in president role, but sub-scale for Summit absent a WI cluster strategy.

name: De Fea Transportation
recent_news: Founded 1964 by the Henze family; Pat DeFea bought the business in 1999 after driving for the Henzes 14 years. 2022 profile: 15 buses (all purchased new 2016-2019), 10 routes over 489 sq mi, ~275 activity trips/yr. Pat noted willingness to sell, preferring local buyer but acknowledging larger-company interest.
fit_notes: Strong succession signal - owner publicly indicated willingness to sell. Two-district SD operator with modern fleet (2016-2019 vintage); meaningful tuck-in candidate if Summit pursues Upper Midwest cluster, though sub-scale standalone. Owner's preference for local buyer is a negotiating consideration.
